changes_summary: Retargeted plan from skill directory to actual source at `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/megaplan/megaplan/`. Added Step 3 to modify `_ensure_verification_task()` in `handlers.py` — the primary root cause of scope creep (FLAG-002). Addressed the artifact model gap (FLAG-003) by having the review agent re-run tests and compare against baseline rather than requiring a new post-execution test schema in `execution.json`. Reordered steps to prioritize the verification task fix as the highest-impact change.
questions: Does _ensure_verification_task() receive the finalize artifacts or the plan metadata as a parameter, or does it need to be wired to load them? Understanding the current function signature determines how baseline data flows in.

Are there other places besides _ensure_verification_task() and prompts/execute.py that encode the 'all tests must pass' policy? (e.g., evaluation.py success-criteria checks, or the orchestrator guidance text)

Is the review agent able to run shell commands (like the test suite) during review, or does it only inspect artifacts and diffs? This determines whether the review-time comparison approach in Step 6 is viable.

success_criteria.criterion: Finalize phase captures a test_baseline.json artifact containing pre-existing test pass/fail status before execution begins
_ensure_verification_task() in handlers.py produces baseline-aware task text that excludes pre-existing failures instead of demanding all tests pass
Executor prompt in prompts/execute.py uses baseline-conditional language when a baseline with failures exists
Review prompt in prompts/review.py instructs the reviewer to evaluate 'no regressions from baseline' instead of 'all tests pass'
Baseline capture gracefully handles: no test command found, test runner errors, timeouts — finalize still completes
All existing megaplan tests pass after changes
When no baseline exists (capture failed/disabled), all three components (verification task, executor prompt, review prompt) preserve their current behavior unchanged
Baseline capture completes within configurable timeout (default 5 minutes)
Baseline feature is configurable (can be disabled, custom test command, custom timeout)
Baseline JSON artifact is human-readable and includes test names, files, and failure status
If pre-existing failures are incidentally fixed during execution, this is reported as a bonus in review, not as required work

assumptions: The megaplan source at /Users/peteromalley/Documents/megaplan/megaplan/ is the editable/development copy used for changes. Step 1 will confirm this.
The _ensure_verification_task() function in handlers.py accepts or can be modified to accept the test baseline data as a parameter.
The review agent can re-run tests during review and compare results against the baseline artifact — this avoids needing to add structured test results to execution.json.
The finalize phase already writes artifacts to .megaplan/<plan>/ and test_baseline.json follows the same pattern.
Pre-existing test failures are the primary source of executor scope creep; other sources (linter warnings, type errors) are out of scope.
Baseline capture is enabled by default (opt-out) since proactive prevention is the goal.
Test detection heuristics (pytest.ini, package.json test script, etc.) are sufficient for v1 — a perfect solution is not needed.
